headline: US Airlines See Robust Demand Even as Consumer Confidence Falls
summary: (Bloomberg) -- American Airlines Group Inc. said it continues to see strong demand for travel, even as surging jet fuel drives up ticket prices and consumer confidence in the US edges down. Rival United Airlines Holdings Inc. also said demand continued to be robust. headline: Marvell, United, Zscaler, and These Stocks Explain the Market Today
summary: Oil prices, meanwhile, continued to decline after Iran’s Revolutionary Guard Corps said the possibility of a return to war with the U.S. was “low.”  Optimism around the U.S. and Iran reaching a peace agreement, despite ongoing hostilities, appeared to be growing.  The chip and tech stocks that have powered the market to record highs in recent weeks were mixed Wednesday.

headline: Airline stocks rally as oil retreat eases fuel cost pressure
summary: Oil is down 8% over the last week as reports circulated that Washington and Tehran were edging closer to an agreement that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz. That drop helped push the S&P 500 and Nasdaq to record closing highs on Tuesday, underscoring the inverse relationship between crude prices and risk assets that has defined markets since the conflict began.

headline: Asian shares are mostly higher, tracking Wall Street's fresh records, and oil prices fall
summary: Asian shares were mostly higher on Wednesday and oil prices fell after the U.S. stock market rose to more records.  It topped 66,000 for the first time in intraday trading.  Computer chip equipment maker Tokyo Electron's shares jumped 5.9% and testing equipment maker Advantest gained 5.7%.
